assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable has full SLA, and distinguishes between minor version timing differences (Rapid gets versions ~2 weeks, Stable gets proven versions after Regular validation)

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: The response does not explicitly state that channel changes don't trigger immediate upgrades or downgrades, though it implies this through the discussion of being 'ahead of channel'

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: Response provides a clear warning about being 'ahead of channel' and that auto-upgrades will stop until Stable catches up to 1.32 (though the expectation mentions 1.30, the principle is correctly addressed for 1.32)

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides the specific command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel stable'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends checking version availability and provides the specific command: 'gcloud container get-server-config --zone YOUR_ZONE --format="yaml(channels.stable.validVersions)"'

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: Response includes a detailed table comparing upgrade cadence and provides clear explanation of how auto-upgrade timing changes from Rapid to Stable channel

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section on Extended channel with 24 months support, manual control, and notes additional cost during extended support period

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed maintenance window configuration commands and recommends applying temporary upgrade freeze during transition, plus includes maintenance window setup examples
